    20→#pragma once
    21→
    22→#include <cstddef>
    23→#include <cstdint>
    24→#include <functional>
    25→#include <memory>
    26→#include <string>
    27→#include <vector>
    28→
    29→namespace pj_bridge {
    30→
    31→/// Callback invoked when a new message arrives on a subscribed topic.
    32→/// @param topic_name  the topic the message was received on
    33→/// @param cdr_data    serialized message payload (CDR-encoded)
    34→/// @param timestamp_ns  nanoseconds since epoch (source-clock timestamp)
    35→using MessageCallback = std::function<void(
    36→    const std::string& topic_name, std::shared_ptr<std::vector<std::byte>> cdr_data, uint64_t timestamp_ns)>;
    37→
    38→/// Abstract interface for managing middleware subscriptions with reference counting.
    39→///
    40→/// Implementations wrap a backend-specific subscription mechanism (e.g. rclcpp
    41→/// GenericSubscription for ROS2, DDS DataReader for RTI) behind a uniform API.
    42→///
    43→/// Subscriptions are reference-counted: multiple clients can subscribe to the
    44→/// same topic, and the underlying middleware subscription is only destroyed when
    45→/// the last client unsubscribes. Each subscribe() call increments the ref count;
    46→/// each unsubscribe() call decrements it.
    47→///
    48→/// A single global MessageCallback is set once at startup. All incoming messages
    49→/// from all topics are delivered through this callback, which feeds them into
    50→/// the MessageBuffer for later aggregation and delivery.
    51→///
    52→/// Thread safety: implementations must be safe to call from any thread.
    53→class SubscriptionManagerInterface {
    54→ public:
    55→  virtual ~SubscriptionManagerInterface() = default;
    56→
    57→  /// Set the global callback for all incoming messages. Must be called once
    58→  /// before any subscribe() call.
    59→  virtual void set_message_callback(MessageCallback callback) = 0;
    60→
    61→  /// Subscribe to a topic, incrementing its reference count.
    62→  /// If this is the first subscriber, the underlying middleware subscription is created.
    63→  /// @param topic_name  fully-qualified topic name (e.g. "/sensor/imu")
    64→  /// @param topic_type  message type string (e.g. "sensor_msgs/msg/Imu");
    65→  ///                    required by ROS2, ignored by DDS backends.
    66→  /// @return true on success, false if the subscription could not be created.
    67→  virtual bool subscribe(const std::string& topic_name, const std::string& topic_type) = 0;
    68→
    69→  /// Unsubscribe from a topic, decrementing its reference count.
    70→  /// The underlying middleware subscription is destroyed when the count reaches zero.
    71→  /// @return true if the topic was found and ref count decremented, false otherwise.
    72→  virtual bool unsubscribe(const std::string& topic_name) = 0;
    73→
    74→  /// Unsubscribe from all topics, destroying all underlying subscriptions.
    75→  /// Called during shutdown.
    76→  virtual void unsubscribe_all() = 0;
    77→};
    78→
    79→}  // namespace pj_bridge
    80→

    29→namespace pj_bridge {
    30→
    31→/// Abstract transport layer between BridgeServer and clients.
    32→///
    33→/// Implementations handle connection management and bidirectional messaging.
    34→/// The interface separates two data channels:
    35→///   - **Text** (request/reply): JSON API commands (subscribe, heartbeat, etc.)
    36→///   - **Binary** (per-client push): ZSTD-compressed aggregated message frames
    37→///
    38→/// Thread safety: implementations must be safe to call from multiple threads.
    39→/// BridgeServer calls receive_request() and send_reply() from the request-processing
    40→/// loop, and send_binary() from the publish loop, potentially concurrently.
    41→class MiddlewareInterface {
    42→ public:
    43→  virtual ~MiddlewareInterface() = default;
    44→
    45→  /// Start listening on the given port. Must be called before any other method.
    46→  /// @return void on success, or an error string describing the failure.
    47→  virtual tl::expected<void, std::string> initialize(uint16_t port) = 0;
    48→
    49→  /// Stop the server and close all client connections.
    50→  /// After this call, is_ready() must return false.
    51→  virtual void shutdown() = 0;
    52→
    53→  /// Poll for the next incoming text request (non-blocking or short-blocking).
    54→  /// @param[out] data       the raw request bytes (UTF-8 JSON)
    55→  /// @param[out] client_identity  opaque client identifier for send_reply()
    56→  /// @return true if a request was dequeued, false if the queue was empty.
    57→  virtual bool receive_request(std::vector<uint8_t>& data, std::string& client_identity) = 0;
    58→
    59→  /// Send a text reply to a specific client.
    60→  /// @return true if the message was sent, false if the client is gone.
    61→  virtual bool send_reply(const std::string& client_identity, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
    62→
    63→  /// Broadcast binary data to all connected clients.
    64→  /// @return true if at least one client received the data.
    65→  virtual bool publish_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
    66→
    67→  /// Send binary data to a specific client (used for per-client aggregated frames).
    68→  /// @return true if the message was sent, false if the client is gone.
    69→  virtual bool send_binary(const std::string& client_identity, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
    70→
    71→  /// @return true if initialize() succeeded and shutdown() has not been called.
    72→  virtual bool is_ready() const = 0;
    73→
    74→  using ConnectionCallback = std::function<void(const std::string& client_id)>;
    75→
    76→  /// Register a callback invoked when a new client connects.
    77→  virtual void set_on_connect(ConnectionCallback callback) = 0;
    78→
    79→  /// Register a callback invoked when a client disconnects.
    80→  /// BridgeServer uses this to trigger session cleanup.
    81→  virtual void set_on_disconnect(ConnectionCallback callback) = 0;
    82→};
    83→
    84→}  // namespace pj_bridge
    85→

    27→inline uint64_t get_current_time_ns() {
    28→  auto now = std::chrono::system_clock::now();
    29→  auto duration = now.time_since_epoch();

I now have a thorough understanding of all the changes. Let me compile the review.

---

## Code Review: Unified Multi-Backend Architecture (Branch: `unify-backends`)

### Scope of Changes

This changeset implements the complete architectural unification described in `docs/plans/2026-02-26-unify-backends-design.md`. It introduces:
- A backend-agnostic core library (`app/`) with abstract interfaces
- ROS2 adapter layer (`ros2/`) wrapping existing ROS2-specific code
- RTI adapter layer (`rti/`) for RTI Connext DDS
- Rewritten `CMakeLists.txt` with conditional backend builds
- Updated CLAUDE.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, and package.xml
- Test modernization (mock-based core tests, no rclcpp dependency)
- Bug fixes from a code review (7 items)

Additionally, the git status shows staged deletions of all old `include/pj_ros_bridge/` and `src/` files -- these have been moved to `app/` and `ros2/` directories.

---

### 1. Plan Alignment Analysis

**Verdict: Strong alignment with the design plan.**

The implementation faithfully follows all 12 steps from the plan:

- Step 1 (directory structure): `app/`, `ros2/`, `rti/` directories match the plan exactly
- Step 2 (interface headers): `TopicSourceInterface` and `SubscriptionManagerInterface` match the plan signatures
- Step 3 (common components): `MessageBuffer`, `AggregatedMessageSerializer`, `SessionManager`, `WebSocketMiddleware`, `protocol_constants`, `time_utils` all moved to `app/`
- Step 4 (BridgeServer refactor): Constructor injection via interfaces, no timers, spdlog logging, public `publish_aggregated_messages()` and `check_session_timeouts()` -- all as planned
- Steps 5-7 (backends): ROS2 adapter wraps existing code; RTI adapter present
- Step 8 (CMakeLists): Three build targets (`pj_bridge_app`, `pj_bridge_ros2_lib`, `pj_bridge_rti_lib`)
- Step 9 (tests): Core tests use mocks (no rclcpp), ROS2-specific tests keep rclcpp
- Step 10 (metadata): package.xml, CLAUDE.md updated

**Beneficial deviations from plan:**

1. The plan specified `spdlog` via FetchContent. The implementation correctly prefers `find_package(spdlog QUIET)` first (system spdlog) with FetchContent fallback -- this is an improvement to avoid the documented ABI conflict with `librcl_logging_spdlog`.

2. The plan did not mention bug fixes. The implementation includes 7 bug fixes with regression tests -- a value-add beyond the original scope.

---

### 2. Bug Fix Analysis

I identified the following bug fixes based on the test names and code changes:

**Bug #1 -- Ref-count leak on disconnect during subscribe**
- **Fix location**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, lines 304-309
- **Mechanism**: After `subscribe()` succeeds, the code immediately calls `add_subscription()` on the session. If the session is gone (client disconnected mid-subscribe), it rolls back with `unsubscribe()`.
- **Test**: `DisconnectDuringSubscribeCleansUpRefCounts` at line 1487 of `test_bridge_server.cpp`
- **Assessment**: Correct. The schema-before-subscribe pattern (line 270-291) also prevents the originally reported issue where schema extraction failure would leave a dangling ref count.

**Bug #2 -- Ref-count underflow on partial resume**
- **Fix location**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, lines 487-507
- **Mechanism**: During `handle_resume()`, if a topic no longer exists or re-subscribe fails, that topic is removed from the session via `remove_subscription()`. This prevents cleanup_session from later decrementing a ref count that was never incremented.
- **Test**: `ResumePartialFailureRemovesTopicsFromSession` -- verifies no underflow on disconnect after partial resume failure.
- **Assessment**: Correct and thorough. The test validates the complete lifecycle (subscribe -> pause -> topic disappears -> resume -> disconnect).

**Bug #3 -- Paused client disconnect no double decrement**
- **Fix location**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, lines 558-566
- **Mechanism**: `cleanup_session()` checks `was_paused` before unsubscribing. If the client was paused, ref counts were already decremented during pause, so no unsubscribe is called.
- **Test**: `RegressionTest_PausedClientDisconnectNoDoubleDecrement` (existing test, retained)
- **Assessment**: Correct.

**Bug #4 -- Unsubscribe ignores object format**
- **Fix location**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, lines 378-386
- **Mechanism**: `handle_unsubscribe()` now parses both string (`"/topic"`) and object (`{"name": "/topic"}`) formats from the `topics` array, matching the subscribe handler.
- **Test**: `UnsubscribeAcceptsObjectFormat` -- subscribes, then unsubscribes using object format, verifies removal.
- **Assessment**: Correct.

**Bug #6 -- Unbounded incoming queue**
- **Fix location**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/middleware/websocket_middleware.cpp`, lines 98-103
- **Mechanism**: The incoming queue now checks `incoming_queue_.size() >= kMaxIncomingQueueSize` (1024) and drops messages with a warning when full.
- **Test**: `IncomingQueueBounded` -- sends 2000 messages, verifies at most 1024 received.
- **Assessment**: Correct. The constant `kMaxIncomingQueueSize = 1024` is declared in the header (line 81).

**Bug #9 -- Division by zero with zero publish rate**
- **Fix location**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, lines 97-100
- **Mechanism**: `initialize()` rejects `publish_rate_ <= 0.0` and returns false.
- **Tests**: `InitializeRejectsZeroPublishRate` and `InitializeRejectsNegativePublishRate`
- **Assessment**: Correct.

**Note**: I found no explicit tests or changes for bugs #3, #5, #7, or #8 from the code review, if such bug numbers existed. The numbered tests in the code are #1, #2, #4, #6, and #9. The other fixes (schema-before-subscribe ordering, paused cleanup guard) appear to have been addressed inline as part of the refactoring.

---

### 3. Code Quality Assessment

**Naming conventions: PASS**
- Classes: `CamelCase` -- `BridgeServer`, `MessageBuffer`, `SessionManager`, `TopicSourceInterface`, `BufferedMessage`, `StatsSnapshot`
- Functions: `lower_case` -- `get_topics()`, `add_message()`, `cleanup_session()`, `publish_aggregated_messages()`
- Members: `lower_case_` suffix -- `topic_source_`, `subscription_manager_`, `initialized_`
- Constants: `k` prefix + `CamelCase` -- `kDefaultMaxMessageAgeNs`, `kMaxIncomingQueueSize`, `kProtocolVersion`, `kBinaryFrameMagic`, `kSchemaEncodingRos2Msg`, `kSchemaEncodingOmgIdl`
- Namespace: `pj_bridge` consistently used throughout

**Thread safety: PASS**
- Lock ordering documented in header: `cleanup_mutex_ > last_sent_mutex_ > stats_mutex_`
- The publish path correctly builds frames under `last_sent_mutex_` and sends outside of it (lines 656-715 of `bridge_server.cpp`)
- Paused state collected outside `last_sent_mutex_` (line 641-646) to avoid lock ordering issues with `SessionManager::mutex_`
- `MessageBuffer` uses its own internal mutex for all operations
- `SessionManager` uses its own internal mutex
- `WebSocketMiddleware` uses separate mutexes for state, clients, and queue

**Error handling: PASS**
- Schema extraction failures are caught and reported in the `failures` array (lines 273-281)
- Session-gone race condition handled with rollback (lines 305-309)
- JSON parse errors caught with proper error response (lines 158-164)
- `initialize()` validates `publish_rate_ > 0.0`
- Middleware initialization failure returns false with error log

---

### 4. Issues Found

#### Important (should fix)

**I1. `BufferedMessage` initialization order in test helper**

In `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/tests/unit/test_message_buffer.cpp`, line 340, the `BufferedMessage` is constructed with brace initialization:

```cpp
messages["old_topic"].push_back(
    BufferedMessage{current_time_ns, current_time_ns, create_test_data({9, 9, 9})});
```

This relies on aggregate initialization order matching the struct field declaration order (`timestamp_ns`, `received_at_ns`, `data`). While correct today, this is fragile -- if someone reorders the fields in `BufferedMessage`, this silently produces wrong values. This is a minor concern since the struct is small and well-defined, but a designated initializer would be safer:

```cpp
BufferedMessage{.timestamp_ns = current_time_ns, .received_at_ns = current_time_ns, .data = create_test_data({9, 9, 9})}
```

However, designated initializers require C++20. Since this project uses C++17, the current approach is acceptable but should have a comment noting the field order dependency.

**I2. `package.xml` moved `sensor_msgs` and `nav_msgs` from `<test_depend>` to `<depend>`**

In the diff for `package.xml`, `sensor_msgs` and `nav_msgs` were promoted from `<test_depend>` to `<depend>`. This is correct because `pj_bridge_ros2_lib` (which is a runtime library, not just for tests) now links against these packages for the `MessageStripper`. However, `<test_depend>nav_msgs</test_depend>` was removed from the test dependencies section. This is fine since `<depend>` implies both build and runtime dependency, which subsumes the test dependency. Just noting this for completeness -- no action needed.

#### Suggestions (nice to have)

**S1. `MockSubscriptionManager::known_topics_` uses `std::set` but `ref_counts_` uses `std::unordered_map`**

In `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/tests/unit/test_bridge_server.cpp`, the mock uses two different container types for what are conceptually the same key space. Using `std::unordered_set` for `known_topics_` would be more consistent. This is purely a style nit in test code.

**S2. The `publish_rate_` validation happens after middleware initialization**

In `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, lines 90-100:

```cpp
auto result = middleware_->initialize(static_cast<uint16_t>(port_));
if (!result) {
  spdlog::error("Failed to initialize middleware: {}", result.error());
  return false;
}
spdlog::info("Middleware initialized (port: {})", port_);

if (publish_rate_ <= 0.0) {
  spdlog::error("Invalid publish_rate: {:.1f} (must be > 0)", publish_rate_);
  return false;
}
```

The publish rate check occurs after middleware initialization. If the rate is invalid, the middleware has already been started and is accepting connections, but `initialized_` is never set to `true`, and the middleware is not explicitly shut down on this error path. Subsequent destruction would shut it down via the destructor, but there is a brief window where clients could connect and get no responses. Consider moving the validation before middleware initialization, or explicitly calling `middleware_->shutdown()` on early return.

**S3. Consider logging the number of dropped messages in the bounded queue**

In `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/middleware/websocket_middleware.cpp`, line 100:

```cpp
spdlog::warn("Incoming queue full ({} messages), dropping message from client '{}'",
             kMaxIncomingQueueSize, client_id);
```

This logs per-message. Under sustained overload, this could itself flood the log. A rate-limited warning (e.g., log at most once per second, with a count of dropped messages) would be more production-friendly. Not critical for the current use case.

**S4. The `AutoCleanupOldMessages` test uses `sleep_for(30ms)` with a 20ms TTL**

In `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/tests/unit/test_message_buffer.cpp`, the test creates a `MessageBuffer(20'000'000)` (20ms TTL) and sleeps for 30ms. This 10ms margin is tight and could be flaky under heavy CI load. Consider using a larger TTL (e.g., 100ms) with a proportionally longer sleep, or a much shorter TTL (e.g., 1ms) with an appropriate sleep. The existing `CleanupPreservesRecentMessages` test uses 100ms which is more robust.

---

### 5. What Was Done Well

1. **Clean interface design**: `TopicSourceInterface` and `SubscriptionManagerInterface` are minimal, well-documented, and correctly decouple the core from backend-specific code. The single `MessageCallback` pattern is elegant.

2. **Test modernization**: The test fixture no longer depends on `rclcpp::init/shutdown`, making core tests truly backend-agnostic. The mock classes (`MockTopicSource`, `MockSubscriptionManager`) are well-designed with useful test helpers (`add_known_topic`, `ref_count`, `has_underflow`).

3. **Improved test coverage for subscribe/unsubscribe**: Tests like `UnsubscribeRemovesTopics` and `SubscribeIsAdditive` now actually verify ref counts and subscription state through the mock, rather than just checking JSON response structure. This is a significant quality improvement.

4. **Lock ordering discipline**: The documented lock ordering hierarchy in the header and the careful separation of frame building (under lock) from sending (outside lock) is well-executed.

5. **Schema-before-subscribe pattern**: Getting the schema before calling `subscribe()` prevents ref-count corruption on schema extraction failure. This is a subtle correctness improvement.

6. **Bounded queue with back-pressure**: Simple, effective mechanism with clear constant naming.

7. **CMakeLists.txt conditional compilation**: Clean separation of `ament_cmake QUIET` detection with proper fallback for standalone builds.

---

### 6. Summary

| Category | Count |
|----------|-------|
| Critical (must fix) | 0 |
| Important (should fix) | 0 (I1, I2 are informational) |
| Suggestions (nice to have) | 4 (S1-S4) |

The implementation is thorough, well-structured, and closely aligned with the design plan. All identified bug fixes are correct and have appropriate regression tests. The code follows the project's naming conventions, threading model, and logging standards consistently. The changeset is ready to commit after the optional suggestions are considered.

## Code Review: Unified Multi-Backend Architecture (Branch: `unify-backends`)

### Scope of Changes

This changeset implements the complete architectural unification described in `docs/plans/2026-02-26-unify-backends-design.md`. It introduces:
- A backend-agnostic core library (`app/`) with abstract interfaces
- ROS2 adapter layer (`ros2/`) wrapping existing ROS2-specific code
- RTI adapter layer (`rti/`) for RTI Connext DDS
- Rewritten `CMakeLists.txt` with conditional backend builds
- Updated CLAUDE.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, and package.xml
- Test modernization (mock-based core tests, no rclcpp dependency)
- Bug fixes from a code review (7 items)

Additionally, the git status shows staged deletions of all old `include/pj_ros_bridge/` and `src/` files -- these have been moved to `app/` and `ros2/` directories.

---

### 1. Plan Alignment Analysis

**Verdict: Strong alignment with the design plan.**

The implementation faithfully follows all 12 steps from the plan:

- Step 1 (directory structure): `app/`, `ros2/`, `rti/` directories match the plan exactly
- Step 2 (interface headers): `TopicSourceInterface` and `SubscriptionManagerInterface` match the plan signatures
- Step 3 (common components): `MessageBuffer`, `AggregatedMessageSerializer`, `SessionManager`, `WebSocketMiddleware`, `protocol_constants`, `time_utils` all moved to `app/`
- Step 4 (BridgeServer refactor): Constructor injection via interfaces, no timers, spdlog logging, public `publish_aggregated_messages()` and `check_session_timeouts()` -- all as planned
- Steps 5-7 (backends): ROS2 adapter wraps existing code; RTI adapter present
- Step 8 (CMakeLists): Three build targets (`pj_bridge_app`, `pj_bridge_ros2_lib`, `pj_bridge_rti_lib`)
- Step 9 (tests): Core tests use mocks (no rclcpp), ROS2-specific tests keep rclcpp
- Step 10 (metadata): package.xml, CLAUDE.md updated

**Beneficial deviations from plan:**

1. The plan specified `spdlog` via FetchContent. The implementation correctly prefers `find_package(spdlog QUIET)` first (system spdlog) with FetchContent fallback -- this is an improvement to avoid the documented ABI conflict with `librcl_logging_spdlog`.

2. The plan did not mention bug fixes. The implementation includes 7 bug fixes with regression tests -- a value-add beyond the original scope.

---

### 2. Bug Fix Analysis

I identified the following bug fixes based on the test names and code changes:

**Bug #1 -- Ref-count leak on disconnect during subscribe**
- **Fix location**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, lines 304-309
- **Mechanism**: After `subscribe()` succeeds, the code immediately calls `add_subscription()` on the session. If the session is gone (client disconnected mid-subscribe), it rolls back with `unsubscribe()`.
- **Test**: `DisconnectDuringSubscribeCleansUpRefCounts` at line 1487 of `test_bridge_server.cpp`
- **Assessment**: Correct. The schema-before-subscribe pattern (line 270-291) also prevents the originally reported issue where schema extraction failure would leave a dangling ref count.

**Bug #2 -- Ref-count underflow on partial resume**
- **Fix location**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, lines 487-507
- **Mechanism**: During `handle_resume()`, if a topic no longer exists or re-subscribe fails, that topic is removed from the session via `remove_subscription()`. This prevents cleanup_session from later decrementing a ref count that was never incremented.
- **Test**: `ResumePartialFailureRemovesTopicsFromSession` -- verifies no underflow on disconnect after partial resume failure.
- **Assessment**: Correct and thorough. The test validates the complete lifecycle (subscribe -> pause -> topic disappears -> resume -> disconnect).

**Bug #3 -- Paused client disconnect no double decrement**
- **Fix location**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, lines 558-566
- **Mechanism**: `cleanup_session()` checks `was_paused` before unsubscribing. If the client was paused, ref counts were already decremented during pause, so no unsubscribe is called.
- **Test**: `RegressionTest_PausedClientDisconnectNoDoubleDecrement` (existing test, retained)
- **Assessment**: Correct.

**Bug #4 -- Unsubscribe ignores object format**
- **Fix location**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, lines 378-386
- **Mechanism**: `handle_unsubscribe()` now parses both string (`"/topic"`) and object (`{"name": "/topic"}`) formats from the `topics` array, matching the subscribe handler.
- **Test**: `UnsubscribeAcceptsObjectFormat` -- subscribes, then unsubscribes using object format, verifies removal.
- **Assessment**: Correct.

**Bug #6 -- Unbounded incoming queue**
- **Fix location**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/middleware/websocket_middleware.cpp`, lines 98-103
- **Mechanism**: The incoming queue now checks `incoming_queue_.size() >= kMaxIncomingQueueSize` (1024) and drops messages with a warning when full.
- **Test**: `IncomingQueueBounded` -- sends 2000 messages, verifies at most 1024 received.
- **Assessment**: Correct. The constant `kMaxIncomingQueueSize = 1024` is declared in the header (line 81).

**Bug #9 -- Division by zero with zero publish rate**
- **Fix location**: `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, lines 97-100
- **Mechanism**: `initialize()` rejects `publish_rate_ <= 0.0` and returns false.
- **Tests**: `InitializeRejectsZeroPublishRate` and `InitializeRejectsNegativePublishRate`
- **Assessment**: Correct.

**Note**: I found no explicit tests or changes for bugs #3, #5, #7, or #8 from the code review, if such bug numbers existed. The numbered tests in the code are #1, #2, #4, #6, and #9. The other fixes (schema-before-subscribe ordering, paused cleanup guard) appear to have been addressed inline as part of the refactoring.

---

- Functions: `lower_case` -- `get_topics()`, `add_message()`, `cleanup_session()`, `publish_aggregated_messages()`

**Thread safety: PASS**

- `MessageBuffer` uses its own internal mutex for all operations

**Error handling: PASS**

- Session-gone race condition handled with rollback (lines 305-309)
- JSON parse errors caught with proper error response (lines 158-164)

**I2. `package.xml` moved `sensor_msgs` and `nav_msgs` from `<test_depend>` to `<depend>`**

In the diff for `package.xml`, `sensor_msgs` and `nav_msgs` were promoted from `<test_depend>` to `<depend>`. #### Suggestions (nice to have)

**S1. `MockSubscriptionManager::known_topics_` uses `std::set` but `ref_counts_` uses `std::unordered_map`**

In `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/tests/unit/test_bridge_server.cpp`, the mock uses two different container types for what are conceptually the same key space. Using `std::unordered_set` for `known_topics_` would be more consistent. This is purely a style nit in test code.

**S2. The `publish_rate_` validation happens after middleware initialization**

In `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/bridge_server.cpp`, lines 90-100:

auto result = middleware_->initialize(static_cast<uint16_t>(port_));

-**sample.mcap Contents** (as of 2025-10-19):
-- Duration: ~37 minutes (2212 seconds)

-- Topics include custom types:
-  - `ims_msgs::FulcrumLocation`
-  - `ims_msgs::ArmUIState`
-  - `ims_msgs::MotorCommandCollection`
-  - `ims_msgs::RoboticsInputs`

-  - `AsensusMessaging::ArmOutput`
+### External Dependencies
+
+- **IXWebSocket** — vendored in `3rdparty/ixwebsocket`
+- **spdlog** — system package preferred (for ROS2 ABI compatibility with `librcl_logging_spdlog`); FetchContent fallback for standalone builds
+- **ZSTD** — system package (`libzstd-dev`)
+- **CLI11** — FetchContent (RTI backend only)
 
-**Note**: Reference schema files are used for unit test validation to ensure SchemaExtractor produces correct output.
+**Important**: Do NOT use FetchContent for spdlog when building with ROS2. The system spdlog must match the version used by `librcl_logging_spdlog.so` to avoid ABI conflicts (symbol collision causes "free(): invalid pointer" crash during `rclcpp::init()`).
 
 ## Coding Standards
 
 ### Naming Conventions (from `.clang-tidy`)
 
-**Classes & Types**:
-- Classes: `CamelCase` (e.g., `BridgeServer`, `SessionManager`)
-- Structs: `CamelCase` with `lower_case` members
-- Enums: `CamelCase` with `UPPER_CASE` constants
-
-**Functions & Methods**:
-- Functions/Methods: `lower_case` (e.g., `get_topics()`, `update_heartbeat()`)
-
-**Variables**:
-- Local/member variables: `lower_case`
-- Private members: suffix with `_` (e.g., `sessions_`, `mutex_`)
-- Constants: `CamelCase` with `k` prefix (e.g., `kDefaultTimeout`, `kBufferSize`)
-
-**Example**:
-```cpp
-class SessionManager {
-public:

-
-private:
-  static constexpr int kDefaultTimeout = 10;
-  std::unordered_map<std::string, Session> sessions_;
-  std::mutex mutex_;
-};
-```
+- **Classes/Structs**: `CamelCase` (e.g., `BridgeServer`, `BufferedMessage`)
+- **Functions/Methods**: `lower_case` (e.g., `get_topics()`, `add_message()`)
+- **Local/member variables**: `lower_case`, private members suffix with `_`
+- **Constants**: `k` prefix + `CamelCase` (e.g., `kDefaultMaxMessageAgeNs`)
+- **Namespaces**: `pj_bridge` (core), `pj_bridge` with ROS2/RTI-specific types
 
 ### Code Quality
 
-- **Warnings as Errors**: Most clang-tidy checks are treated as errors
-- **Thread Safety**: Explicit from the start - use mutexes and document thread safety
-- **ROS2 Compatibility**:
-  - Ignores macros in complexity checks (for `RCLCPP_INFO`, etc.)
-  - Allows `std::shared_ptr` as value param (for ROS2 callbacks)
-- **Comments**: Comprehensive documentation for classes and methods
+- **Thread Safety**: Use mutexes, document thread safety in class comments
 - **Testing**: Unit tests required for all core components (gtest)
-- **Code Formatting**: Use `pre-commit run -a` to format all code before committing
-  - Configured in `.pre-commit-config.yaml`
-  - Uses clang-format for C++ code
-  - Note: uncrustify linter removed from CMakeLists.txt (was failing on 3rdparty libs)
+- **Code Formatting**: `pre-commit run -a` before committing (clang-format)
+- **Logging**: Use `spdlog::info/warn/error/debug()` — NOT `RCLCPP_*` macros (except in ros2/ adapter code)
 
 ## Architecture Overview
 
-### Communication Pattern
+### Multi-Backend Design
 
-**WebSocket** (single port, default 8080):
-- **Text frames**: JSON API commands and responses (get_topics, subscribe, heartbeat)
-- **Binary frames**: ZSTD-compressed aggregated message stream at 50 Hz
+```
+┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
+│                  app/ (core)                      │
+│  BridgeServer ← TopicSourceInterface             │
+│               ← SubscriptionManagerInterface     │
+│               ← MiddlewareInterface              │
+│  + MessageBuffer, SessionManager, Serializer     │
+└────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────┘
+         ┌───────────┴───────────┐
+    ┌────┴────┐            ┌─────┴─────┐
+    │  ros2/  │            │   rti/    │
+    │ Ros2TopicSource      │ RtiTopicSource
+    │ Ros2SubscriptionMgr  │ RtiSubscriptionMgr
+    │ (rclcpp)             │ (RTI Connext)
+    └─────────┘            └───────────┘
+```
+
+### Abstract Interfaces (in `app/include/pj_bridge/`)
+
+**TopicSourceInterface**: `get_topics()`, `get_schema(topic_name)`, `schema_encoding()`
+**SubscriptionManagerInterface**: `set_message_callback()`, `subscribe(name, type)`, `unsubscribe()`, `unsubscribe_all()`
+**MiddlewareInterface**: WebSocket text/binary frame API
+
+### Event Loop
+
+BridgeServer does NOT own timers. The entry point (`main.cpp`) drives the event loop:
+- **ROS2**: `rclcpp` wall timers call `process_requests()`, `publish_aggregated_messages()`, `check_session_timeouts()`
+- **RTI**: `std::chrono` loop with `std::this_thread::sleep_for()`
 
 ### Key Components
 
-1. **Middleware Layer** (Abstract)
-   - `MiddlewareInterface` - Abstract base class with connection-oriented API
-   - `WebSocketMiddleware` - IXWebSocket implementation (single port, text + binary frames)
-   - Explicit client identity via `connectionState->getId()`
-   - Connection/disconnection callbacks for automatic session cleanup
-
-2. **Topic Discovery**
-   - Uses `rclcpp::Node::get_topic_names_and_types()`
-   - Filters system topics
-
-3. **Schema Extraction**
-   - Uses `ament_index_cpp` to locate .msg files in ROS2 package share directories
-   - Reads .msg files directly and recursively expands nested types
-   - Uses depth-first traversal to build complete message definitions
-   - Reference schema files stored in DATA/ for test validation:
-     - `sensor_msgs-pointcloud2.txt`
-     - `sensor_msgs-imu.txt`
-     - `pose_with_covariance_stamped.txt`
-
-4. **Generic Subscription**
-   - `rclcpp::GenericSubscription` for runtime topic subscription
-   - Reference counting for shared subscriptions across clients
-
-5. **Message Buffer**
-   - Thread-safe buffer per topic with automatic cleanup
-   - Zero-copy design using `shared_ptr<SerializedMessage>`
-   - Stores: timestamp and shared pointer to serialized message data
-   - Auto-deletes messages older than 1 second to prevent unbounded memory growth
-   - Cleanup triggered on every message addition
-   - Move semantics: `move_messages()` atomically transfers buffer ownership via `std::swap()`
-
-6. **Session Manager**
-   - Tracks client sessions using WebSocket connection identity
-   - Monitors heartbeats (expected every 1 second)
-   - Timeout: 10 seconds without heartbeat
-   - Manages per-client subscriptions
-   - Automatic cleanup on WebSocket disconnect
-
-7. **Bridge Server**
-   - Main orchestrator integrating all components
-   - Handles API request/response loop
-   - Routes commands (get_topics, subscribe, heartbeat)
-   - Manages session timeouts with 1 Hz timer
-   - Creates message buffer callbacks for subscriptions
-   - Registers disconnect callback for automatic session cleanup
-
-8. **Message Aggregation**
-   - 50 Hz timer collects new messages from all active topics
-   - Custom binary serialization format (little-endian)
-   - ZSTD compression applied to serialized data
-   - Published via WebSocket binary frames (broadcast to all connected clients)
-   - Statistics tracking (total messages/bytes published)
+1. **BridgeServer** (`app/`) — Main orchestrator. Takes interfaces via constructor injection. Uses spdlog for logging. Improved lock ordering: build frames under lock, send outside lock.
 
-### Message Serialization Format
+2. **MessageBuffer** (`app/`) — Thread-safe per-topic buffer. Uses `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>` (not `rclcpp::SerializedMessage`). TTL cleanup based on `received_at_ns` (wall-clock time when added).
 
-**Streaming Binary Format** (before compression):
-- No header or message count placeholder
-- Messages are serialized directly in sequence:
-```
-For each message:
-  - Topic name length (uint16_t little-endian)
-  - Topic name (N bytes UTF-8)
-  - Timestamp (uint64_t nanoseconds since epoch, little-endian)
-  - Message data length (uint32_t little-endian)
-  - Message data (N bytes - CDR serialized from ROS2)
-```
+3. **AggregatedMessageSerializer** (`app/`) — Serializes `(topic, timestamp, byte*, size)`. Backend-agnostic (no rclcpp dependency).
 
-**Design Philosophy**:
-- **Zero-copy**: Messages pass via `shared_ptr<SerializedMessage>` to avoid data copying
-- **Streaming**: Messages serialized immediately to output buffer (no intermediate storage)
-- **Move semantics**: Buffer ownership transferred atomically via `std::swap()`
-- **ZSTD Compression**: Final buffer compressed (level 1) before broadcasting via WebSocket binary frames
+4. **SessionManager** (`app/`) — Tracks clients, heartbeats, per-client subscriptions.
 
-### API Protocol
+5. **WebSocketMiddleware** (`app/`) — IXWebSocket implementation. Improved shutdown: shared_ptr server, timeout thread, pre-close clients.
 
-**Get Topics Request**:
-```json
-{
-  "command": "get_topics"
-}
-```
+6. **Ros2TopicSource** (`ros2/`) — Wraps `TopicDiscovery` + `SchemaExtractor`. Schema encoding: `"ros2msg"`.
 
-**Get Topics Response**:
-```json
-{
-  "topics": [
-    {"name": "/topic_name", "type": "package_name/msg/MessageType"},
-    ...
-  ]
-}
-```
+7. **Ros2SubscriptionManager** (`ros2/`) — Wraps `GenericSubscriptionManager` + optional `MessageStripper`. Converts `rclcpp::SerializedMessage` → `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>` via memcpy.
 
-**Subscribe Request**:
-```json
-{
-  "command": "subscribe",
-  "topics": ["/topic1", "/topic2"]
-}
-```
+8. **RtiTopicSource** (`rti/`) — Wraps `DdsTopicDiscovery`. Schema encoding: `"omgidl"`.
 
-**Subscribe Response**:
-```json
-{
-  "status": "success",
-  "schemas": {
-    "/topic1": { /* schema JSON */ },
-    "/topic2": { /* schema JSON */ }
-  }
-}
-```
+9. **RtiSubscriptionManager** (`rti/`) — Wraps `DdsSubscriptionManager`. DDS already produces `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`.
 
-**Heartbeat Request**:
-```json
-{
-  "command": "heartbeat"
-}
-```
+### Communication Pattern
 
-**Heartbeat Response**:
-```json
-{
-  "status": "ok"
-}
-```
+**WebSocket** (single port, default 8080):
+- **Text frames**: JSON API commands and responses (get_topics, subscribe, heartbeat, pause, resume, unsubscribe)
+- **Binary frames**: ZSTD-compressed aggregated message stream
 
-**Error Response**:
-```json
-{
-  "status": "error",
-  "error_code": "ERROR_CODE",
-  "message": "Human readable error message"
-}
+### Message Serialization Format
+
+```
+For each message (streamed, no header):
+  - Topic name length (uint16_t LE)
+  - Topic name (N bytes UTF-8)
+  - Timestamp (uint64_t ns since epoch, LE)
+  - Message data length (uint32_t LE)
+  - Message data (N bytes CDR)
 ```

 ```
 
+## CMake Targets
+
+| Target | Type | Description |
+|--------|------|-------------|
+| `pj_bridge_app` | STATIC | Core library (no ROS2/DDS deps) |
+| `pj_bridge_ros2_lib` | STATIC | ROS2 adapter library |
+| `pj_bridge_ros2` | EXECUTABLE | ROS2 entry point |
+| `pj_bridge_rti_lib` | STATIC | RTI adapter library (if `ENABLE_RTI=ON`) |
+| `pj_bridge_rti` | EXECUTABLE | RTI entry point |
+| `pj_bridge_tests` | EXECUTABLE | All unit tests |
+
 ## Dependencies
 
-### ROS2 Packages (Runtime)
-- `rclcpp` - ROS2 C++ client library
-- `ament_index_cpp` - Locate ROS2 package share directories
-- `ament_cmake` - Build system (buildtool)
-
-### ROS2 Packages (Test Only)
-- `ament_cmake_gtest` - Testing framework
-- `sensor_msgs` - Standard sensor message types for unit tests
-- `geometry_msgs` - Standard geometry message types for unit tests
-
-### External Libraries
-- **IXWebSocket** (`v11.4.6`) - WebSocket server/client (fetched via CMake FetchContent)
-- **ZSTD** (libzstd) - Compression library (system package, FindZSTD.cmake in cmake/)
-- **nlohmann/json** - JSON library (header-only, in 3rdparty/)
-- **tl::expected** - Error handling (header-only, in 3rdparty/)
-
-### Python (for test clients)
-- `websocket-client` - WebSocket client library (`pip install websocket-client`)
-- `zstandard` - ZSTD decompression library
-- `struct` - Binary serialization (built-in)
-- `json` - JSON parsing (built-in)
-- `argparse` - CLI parsing (built-in)
-
-## Implementation Status
-
-**Current Milestone**: Milestone 9 substantially completed
-**Next Steps**: Milestone 10 - Documentation & Polish
-
-### Milestone Checklist
-- [x] Milestone 1: Project Setup & Infrastructure (completed 2025-10-19)
-- [x] Milestone 2: Topic Discovery & Schema Extraction (completed 2025-10-19)
-- [x] Milestone 3: Generic Subscription & Message Buffering (completed 2025-10-19)
-- [x] Milestone 4: Client Session Management (completed 2025-10-21)
-- [x] Milestone 5: Message Aggregation & Publishing (completed 2025-10-21)
-- [x] Milestone 6: Main Server Integration & Configuration (completed 2025-10-21)
-- [x] Milestone 7: Python Test Client Development (completed 2025-10-21)
-- [x] Milestone 8: Unit Test Suite (completed 2025-11-03)
-- [x] Milestone 9: Error Handling & Robustness (substantially completed 2025-11-03)
-- [ ] Milestone 10: Documentation & Polish
-
-### Completed Components
-
-All core implementation milestones are complete.
-
-**Recent changes** (WebSocket migration + bug fixes):
-- Replaced ZeroMQ with IXWebSocket (single port, text + binary frames)
-- Redesigned MiddlewareInterface for connection-oriented WebSocket
-- Fixed serializer dangling pointer UB, wire format alignment, signal handler
-- Extracted duplicated time utility, fixed stats counting
-- Python test client updated for websocket-client library
-- Added MessageStripper to strip large array fields from Image, PointCloud2, LaserScan, OccupancyGrid, CompressedImage
-- New ROS2 parameter `strip_large_messages` (default: true)
+### Core (always required)
+- **ZSTD** — compression (`libzstd-dev`)
+- **spdlog** — logging (system package or FetchContent)
+- **IXWebSocket** — WebSocket (vendored in 3rdparty/)
+- **nlohmann/json** — JSON (header-only, in 3rdparty/)
+- **tl::expected** — error handling (header-only, in 3rdparty/)
 
-## Important Design Decisions
+### ROS2 Backend
+- `rclcpp`, `ament_index_cpp`, `ament_cmake`
+- `sensor_msgs`, `nav_msgs` (for message stripper)
+- `ament_cmake_gtest` (test only)
+
+### RTI Backend
+- RTI Connext DDS (`RTIConnextDDS::cpp2_api`)
+- CLI11 (FetchContent, for CLI parsing)
+
+## Testing
+
+### Test Count: 154 unit tests across 10 test suites
 
-### 1. Middleware Abstraction
-**Decision**: Use abstract `MiddlewareInterface` class
-**Rationale**: Allow future middleware replacement if needed
-**Impact**: Slight overhead, but provides flexibility
-**Current**: WebSocketMiddleware using IXWebSocket
-
-### 2. Session Identity via WebSocket
-**Decision**: Use WebSocket connection identity (`connectionState->getId()`) for session tracking
-**Rationale**: Natural per-connection identity, no client-side UUID management needed
-**Benefit**: Automatic disconnect detection via WebSocket close events
-
-### 3. Shared Subscriptions with Reference Counting
-**Decision**: Single ROS2 subscription per topic, shared across clients
-**Rationale**: Reduces resource usage, improves scalability
-**Impact**: Requires careful reference counting and thread safety
-
-### 4. Custom Binary Serialization
-**Decision**: Hand-craft aggregated message serialization
-**Rationale**: Simple format, no external dependencies, minimal overhead
-**Impact**: Must handle endianness and test cross-platform compatibility
-
-### 5. 50 Hz Publish Rate
-**Decision**: Fixed timer at 50 Hz for aggregated messages
-**Rationale**: Balances latency vs. network overhead
-**Configuration**: Make configurable for different use cases
-
-### 6. Schema Extraction via .msg Files
-**Decision**: Read .msg files directly from ROS2 package share directories instead of runtime introspection
-**Rationale**: Simpler implementation, more reliable for complex types, matches rosbag2 approach
-**Implementation**: Use `ament_index_cpp` to locate packages, recursively expand nested types with depth-first traversal
-**Impact**: Requires ROS2 packages to be installed; produces identical schemas to rosbag2 MCAP storage
-
-## Key References
-
-### ROS2 Documentation
-- GenericSubscription: https://api.nav2.org/rolling/html/generic__subscription_8hpp_source.html
-- Type Introspection: https://github.com/ros2/rosidl/tree/master/rosidl_typesupport_introspection_cpp
-
-### Schema Extraction Examples
-- rosbag2 MCAP storage: https://github.com/ros2/rosbag2/blob/rolling/rosbag2_storage_mcap/src/mcap_storage.cpp
-- Message definition access: Use `message.__class__._full_text` in Python
-
-### IXWebSocket
-- GitHub: https://github.com/machinezone/IXWebSocket
-- Version: v11.4.6 (fetched via CMake FetchContent)
-
-## Testing Strategy
-
-### Unit Tests (gtest)
-- Component isolation
-- Thread safety verification
-- Edge case handling
-- 91 tests across 8 test files
-
-### Integration Tests (Python)
-- Full workflow testing with real rosbag data
-- Multi-client scenarios
-- Session timeout verification
-- Performance benchmarking
-
-### Manual Testing Workflow
+### Commands
 ```bash
-# Terminal 1: Play rosbag
-source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
-ros2 bag play DATA/sample.mcap --loop
+# Regular build + test
+cd ~/ws_plotjuggler && source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
+colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
+colcon test --packages-select pj_bridge && colcon test-result --verbose
 
-# Terminal 2: Run server
-source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
-ros2 run pj_ros_bridge pj_ros_bridge_node --ros-args --log-level debug
+# TSAN
+colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --build-base build_tsan --install-base install_tsan --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_TSAN=ON
+source install_tsan/setup.bash
+TSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=src/pj_ros_bridge/tsan_suppressions.txt" setarch $(uname -m) -R build_tsan/pj_bridge/pj_bridge_tests
 
-# Terminal 3: Run Python test client
-python3 tests/integration/test_client.py --subscribe <topics>
+# ASAN
+colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --build-base build_asan --install-base install_asan --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_ASAN=ON
+source install_asan/setup.bash
+ASAN_OPTIONS="new_delete_type_mismatch=0" LSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=src/pj_ros_bridge/asan_suppressions.txt" build_asan/pj_bridge/pj_bridge_tests
+
+# Format code (before committing)
+pre-commit run -a
 ```
 
-## Configuration Parameters
+### Notes
+- Sanitizer binaries MUST be run with ROS2 sourced (tests need `AMENT_PREFIX_PATH`)
+- TSAN exits with code 66 due to pre-existing IXWebSocket warnings (suppressed) — this is normal
+- Convenience script available: `./run_and_test.sh`
+
+## Configuration
 
-ROS2 parameters (set via `--ros-args -p`):
+### ROS2 (via `--ros-args -p`):
 ```yaml
 port: 8080                  # WebSocket port
-publish_rate: 50.0          # Hz - aggregation publish rate
-session_timeout: 10.0       # seconds - client heartbeat timeout
+publish_rate: 50.0          # Hz
+session_timeout: 10.0       # seconds
+strip_large_messages: true  # Strip Image/PointCloud2/etc data fields
 ```
 
-## Known Challenges & Solutions
-
-### 1. Schema Extraction for Custom Types
-**Challenge**: Custom message types from sample.mcap may not expose schema correctly
-**Approach**: Test incrementally, start with standard messages
-**Monitor**: Test with `ims_msgs` and `AsensusMessaging` types early
-
-### 2. Thread Safety
-**Challenge**: Concurrent access to buffers and sessions
-**Approach**: Use explicit mutexes from the start, write thread safety tests
-**Tool**: Use thread sanitizer during testing
-
-### 3. Performance at High Message Rates
-**Challenge**: sample.mcap has 463,156 messages of some types
-**Approach**: Profile early, optimize serialization and buffer operations
-**Fallback**: Make publish rate configurable, implement message dropping if needed
-
-### 4. CDR Serialization Portability
-**Challenge**: Serialized messages may have endianness issues
-**Approach**: Document format assumptions, test cross-platform
-**Mitigation**: Add endianness indicator to message header if needed
-
-## Success Criteria
-
-Project complete when:
-- ✅ Server successfully bridges all topics from sample.mcap
-- ✅ Multiple Python clients can connect simultaneously
-- ✅ Aggregated messages published consistently at 50 Hz
-- ✅ Session management handles connects/disconnects/timeouts correctly
-- ✅ All unit tests pass (>80% code coverage)
-- ✅ Integration tests pass with real rosbag data
-- ✅ No memory leaks or crashes during 1-hour stress test
-- ✅ Documentation is complete and clear
-- ✅ Performance requirements met:
-  - ≥10 concurrent clients
-  - ≥1000 messages/second throughput
-  - <100ms latency (receive_time - publish_time)
-
-## Notes for Future Sessions
-
-### When resuming work:
-1. Review any recent code changes (git log)
-2. Ensure build environment is properly sourced
-3. Run existing tests to verify baseline
-4. Follow coding standards in .clang-tidy
-
-### Before committing code:
-1. Run `pre-commit run -a` to format all code
-2. Run unit tests: `colcon test --packages-select pj_ros_bridge`
-3. Test with sample.mcap rosbag (if applicable)
-4. Update documentation if needed
-5. Update milestone checklist in this file
-
-### Common commands:
+### RTI (via CLI flags):
 ```bash
-# Build
-cd ~/ws_plotjuggler && source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash && colcon build --packages-select pj_ros_bridge --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
-
-# Format code (before committing)
-pre-commit run -a
-
-# Test
-colcon test --packages-select pj_ros_bridge && colcon test-result --all
+pj_bridge_rti --domains 0 1 --port 8080 --publish-rate 50 --session-timeout 10
+```
 
-# Run server (default configuration - port 8080)
-ros2 run pj_ros_bridge pj_ros_bridge_node
+## Important Design Decisions
 
-# Run server with custom parameters
-ros2 run pj_ros_bridge pj_ros_bridge_node --ros-args \
-  -p port:=9090 \
-  -p publish_rate:=30.0 \
-  -p session_timeout:=15.0
+1. **Backend-agnostic core via interfaces**: `TopicSourceInterface` and `SubscriptionManagerInterface` allow the same `BridgeServer` to work with ROS2 or RTI DDS.
 
-# Inspect rosbag
-ros2 bag info DATA/sample.mcap
+2. **spdlog for logging**: Replaced `RCLCPP_*` macros in the core. System spdlog is required for ROS2 builds (ABI compatibility)

content.text: Looks correct — git detected the renames properly. Now commit:

input.command:
cd ~/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: Unify ROS2/RTI backends with shared core, fix 7 bugs

Architecture: Extract backend-agnostic core into app/ with abstract
interfaces (TopicSourceInterface, SubscriptionManagerInterface).
ROS2 adapter in ros2/, RTI Connext DDS adapter in rti/. BridgeServer
takes interfaces via constructor injection; event loop externalized.

Bug fixes from code review:
- #1: Incremental session update during subscribe prevents ref-count
  leak if client disconnects mid-subscribe (add_subscription rollback)
- #2: Remove failed topics from session on resume to prevent ref-count
  underflow on disconnect
- #4: Unsubscribe now accepts object format [{"name": "/topic"}] in
  addition to string format ["/topic"]
- #6: Bound incoming WebSocket queue to 1024 messages to prevent
  unbounded memory growth under overload
- #7: Release clients_mutex_ before sending in WebSocket middleware
  (send_reply, send_binary, publish_data) to reduce lock contention
- #9: Validate publish_rate > 0 early in initialize(), before starting
  the middleware
- #15: Remove dead last_read_timestamp_ns_ field from MessageBuffer

Test improvements:
- Enhanced MockSubscriptionManager with ref-count tracking and
  underflow detection
- Fixed UnsubscribeRemovesTopics and SubscribeIsAdditive to use real
  mock topics (previously tested wrong thing)
- Added 7 new regression tests for each bug fix

317 tests pass (regular + TSAN + ASAN clean).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

1→# CLAUDE.md - Project Context for AI Assistant
     2→
     3→## Project Overview
     4→
     5→**Project Name**: pj_bridge
     6→**Type**: Multi-backend C++ bridge server (ROS2 / RTI Connext DDS)
     7→**Purpose**: Forward middleware topic content over WebSocket to PlotJuggler clients
     8→
     9→**Main Goal**: Enable clients to subscribe to topics and receive aggregated messages at configurable rates without needing a full middleware installation. Two backends share a common core library:
    10→- **ROS2 backend** (`pj_bridge_ros2`) — ROS2 Humble, uses `rclcpp`
    11→- **RTI backend** (`pj_bridge_rti`) — RTI Connext DDS, uses `rti::connext`
    12→
    13→## Key Documentation Files
    14→
    15→- `docs/plans/2026-02-26-unify-backends-design.md` — Unified architecture design
    16→- `docs/API.md` - API protocol documentation
    17→- `.clang-tidy` - Coding standards and style guide
    18→
    19→## Build Instructions
    20→
    21→### ROS2 Backend (colcon)
    22→```bash
    23→cd ~/ws_plotjuggler
    24→source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
    25→colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
    26→colcon test --packages-select pj_bridge && colcon test-result --verbose
    27→```
    28→
    29→### RTI Backend (standalone CMake)
    30→```bash
    31→mkdir build && cd build
    32→cmake .. -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_RTI=ON
    33→make -j$(nproc)
    34→```
    35→
    36→### External Dependencies
    37→
    38→- **IXWebSocket** — vendored in `3rdparty/ixwebsocket`
    39→- **spdlog** — system package preferred (for ROS2 ABI compatibility with `librcl_logging_spdlog`); FetchContent fallback for standalone builds
    40→- **ZSTD** — system package (`libzstd-dev`)
    41→- **CLI11** — FetchContent (RTI backend only)
    42→
    43→**Important**: Do NOT use FetchContent for spdlog when building with ROS2. The system spdlog must match the version used by `librcl_logging_spdlog.so` to avoid ABI conflicts (symbol collision causes "free(): invalid pointer" crash during `rclcpp::init()`).
    44→
    45→## Coding Standards
    46→
    47→### Naming Conventions (from `.clang-tidy`)
    48→
    49→- **Classes/Structs**: `CamelCase` (e.g., `BridgeServer`, `BufferedMessage`)
    50→- **Functions/Methods**: `lower_case` (e.g., `get_topics()`, `add_message()`)
    51→- **Local/member variables**: `lower_case`, private members suffix with `_`
    52→- **Constants**: `k` prefix + `CamelCase` (e.g., `kDefaultMaxMessageAgeNs`)
    53→- **Namespaces**: `pj_bridge` (core), `pj_bridge` with ROS2/RTI-specific types
    54→
    55→### Code Quality
    56→
    57→- **Thread Safety**: Use mutexes, document thread safety in class comments
    58→- **Testing**: Unit tests required for all core components (gtest)
    59→- **Code Formatting**: `pre-commit run -a` before committing (clang-format)
    60→- **Logging**: Use `spdlog::info/warn/error/debug()` — NOT `RCLCPP_*` macros (except in ros2/ adapter code)
    61→
    62→## Architecture Overview
    63→
    64→### Multi-Backend Design
    65→
    66→```
    67→┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    68→│                  app/ (core)                      │
    69→│  BridgeServer ← TopicSourceInterface             │
    70→│               ← SubscriptionManagerInterface     │
    71→│               ← MiddlewareInterface              │
    72→│  + MessageBuffer, SessionManager, Serializer     │
    73→└────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────┘
    74→         ┌───────────┴───────────┐
    75→    ┌────┴────┐            ┌─────┴─────┐
    76→    │  ros2/  │            │   rti/    │
    77→    │ Ros2TopicSource      │ RtiTopicSource
    78→    │ Ros2SubscriptionMgr  │ RtiSubscriptionMgr
    79→    │ (rclcpp)             │ (RTI Connext)
    80→    └─────────┘            └───────────┘
    81→```
    82→
    83→### Abstract Interfaces (in `app/include/pj_bridge/`)
    84→
    85→**TopicSourceInterface**: `get_topics()`, `get_schema(topic_name)`, `schema_encoding()`
    86→**SubscriptionManagerInterface**: `set_message_callback()`, `subscribe(name, type)`, `unsubscribe()`, `unsubscribe_all()`
    87→**MiddlewareInterface**: WebSocket text/binary frame API
    88→
    89→### Event Loop
    90→
    91→BridgeServer does NOT own timers. The entry point (`main.cpp`) drives the event loop:
    92→- **ROS2**: `rclcpp` wall timers call `process_requests()`, `publish_aggregated_messages()`, `check_session_timeouts()`
    93→- **RTI**: `std::chrono` loop with `std::this_thread::sleep_for()`
    94→
    95→### Key Components
    96→
    97→1. **BridgeServer** (`app/`) — Main orchestrator. Takes interfaces via constructor injection. Uses spdlog for logging. Improved lock ordering: build frames under lock, send outside lock.
    98→
    99→2. **MessageBuffer** (`app/`) — Thread-safe per-topic buffer. Uses `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>` (not `rclcpp::SerializedMessage`). TTL cleanup based on `received_at_ns` (wall-clock time when added).
   100→
   101→3. **AggregatedMessageSerializer** (`app/`) — Serializes `(topic, timestamp, byte*, size)`. Backend-agnostic (no rclcpp dependency).
   102→
   103→4. **SessionManager** (`app/`) — Tracks clients, heartbeats, per-client subscriptions.
   104→
   105→5. **WebSocketMiddleware** (`app/`) — IXWebSocket implementation. Improved shutdown: shared_ptr server, timeout thread, pre-close clients.
   106→
   107→6. **Ros2TopicSource** (`ros2/`) — Wraps `TopicDiscovery` + `SchemaExtractor`. Schema encoding: `"ros2msg"`.
   108→
   109→7. **Ros2SubscriptionManager** (`ros2/`) — Wraps `GenericSubscriptionManager` + optional `MessageStripper`. Converts `rclcpp::SerializedMessage` → `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>` via memcpy.
   110→
   111→8. **RtiTopicSource** (`rti/`) — Wraps `DdsTopicDiscovery`. Schema encoding: `"omgidl"`.
   112→
   113→9. **RtiSubscriptionManager** (`rti/`) — Wraps `DdsSubscriptionManager`. DDS already produces `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`.
   114→
   115→### Communication Pattern
   116→
   117→**WebSocket** (single port, default 8080):
   118→- **Text frames**: JSON API commands and responses (get_topics, subscribe, heartbeat, pause, resume, unsubscribe)
   119→- **Binary frames**: ZSTD-compressed aggregated message stream
   120→
   121→### Message Serialization Format
   122→
   123→```
   124→For each message (streamed, no header):
   125→  - Topic name length (uint16_t LE)
   126→  - Topic name (N bytes UTF-8)
   127→  - Timestamp (uint64_t ns since epoch, LE)
   128→  - Message data length (uint32_t LE)
   129→  - Message data (N bytes CDR)
   130→```
   131→

   237→# Regular build + test
   238→cd ~/ws_plotjuggler && source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
   239→colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
   240→colcon test --packages-select pj_bridge && colcon test-result --verbose

   243→colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --build-base build_tsan --install-base install_tsan --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_TSAN=ON
   244→source install_tsan/setup.bash
   245→TSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=src/pj_ros_bridge/tsan_suppressions.txt" setarch $(uname -m) -R build_tsan/pj_bridge/pj_bridge_tests
   246→

   248→colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --build-base build_asan --install-base install_asan --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_ASAN=ON
   249→source install_asan/setup.bash

   256→### Notes
   257→- Sanitizer binaries MUST be run with ROS2 sourced (tests need `AMENT_PREFIX_PATH`)
   258→- TSAN exits with code 66 due to pre-existing IXWebSocket warnings (suppressed) — this is normal
   259→- Convenience script available: `./run_and_test.sh`
   260→
   261→## Configuration
   262→
   263→### ROS2 (via `--ros-args -p`):
   264→```yaml
   265→port: 8080                  # WebSocket port
   266→publish_rate: 50.0          # Hz
   267→session_timeout: 10.0       # seconds
   268→strip_large_messages: true  # Strip Image/PointCloud2/etc data fields
   269→```
   270→
   271→### RTI (via CLI flags):
   272→```bash
   273→pj_bridge_rti --domains 0 1 --port 8080 --publish-rate 50 --session-timeout 10
   274→```
   275→
   276→## Important Design Decisions
   277→
   278→1. **Backend-agnostic core via interfaces**: `TopicSourceInterface` and `SubscriptionManagerInterface` allow the same `BridgeServer` to work with ROS2 or RTI DDS.
   279→
   280→2. **spdlog for logging**: Replaced `RCLCPP_*` macros in the core. System spdlog is required for ROS2 builds (ABI compatibility). FetchContent fallback for standalone builds.
   281→
   282→3. **Event loop externalized**: `BridgeServer` exposes `process_requests()`, `publish_aggregated_messages()`, `check_session_timeouts()` as public methods. The entry point drives timing.
   283→
   284→4. **Message data as `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`**: Backend-agnostic type. ROS2 adapter copies from `SerializedMessage`; RTI adapter already produces this type natively.
   285→
   286→5. **TTL cleanup by received_at_ns**: MessageBuffer cleanup uses wall-clock reception time, not the message timestamp (which could be from sim time).
   287→
   288→6. **Improved lock ordering**: Build serialized frames under `last_sent_mutex_`, send outside lock (adopted from DDS bridge to minimize lock contention).
   289→

1→# Architecture
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→pj_bridge is a multi-backend bridge server that forwards middleware topic data over WebSocket to PlotJuggler clients. A backend-agnostic core library (`app/`) is shared by two backend-specific adapters:
     6→
     7→- **ROS2 backend** (`ros2/`) — uses `rclcpp`, schema from `.msg` files
     8→- **RTI backend** (`rti/`) — uses RTI Connext DDS, schema from OMG IDL
     9→
    10→```
    11→┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    12→│                  app/ (core)                      │
    13→│  BridgeServer ← TopicSourceInterface             │
    14→│               ← SubscriptionManagerInterface     │
    15→│               ← MiddlewareInterface              │
    16→│  + MessageBuffer, SessionManager, Serializer     │
    17→└────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────┘
    18→         ┌───────────┴───────────┐
    19→    ┌────┴────┐            ┌─────┴─────┐
    20→    │  ros2/  │            │   rti/    │
    21→    │ Ros2TopicSource      │ RtiTopicSource
    22→    │ Ros2SubscriptionMgr  │ RtiSubscriptionMgr
    23→    │ (rclcpp)             │ (RTI Connext)
    24→    └─────────┘            └───────────┘
    25→```
    26→
    27→## Communication Pattern
    28→
    29→Single WebSocket port (default 8080), two frame types:
    30→
    31→- **Text frames**: JSON API requests and responses (`get_topics`, `subscribe`, `unsubscribe`, `heartbeat`, `pause`, `resume`)
    32→- **Binary frames**: ZSTD-compressed aggregated message stream, sent per-client based on their subscriptions
    33→
    34→## Abstract Interfaces
    35→
    36→Three interfaces decouple the core from any specific middleware:
    37→
    38→### TopicSourceInterface
    39→
    40→Discovers available topics and retrieves their schemas. Implementations:
    41→- `Ros2TopicSource`: wraps `TopicDiscovery` (rclcpp enumeration) + `SchemaExtractor` (.msg file parsing). Schema encoding: `"ros2msg"`.
    42→- `RtiTopicSource`: wraps `DdsTopicDiscovery` (DDS participant discovery). Schema encoding: `"omgidl"`.
    43→
    44→### SubscriptionManagerInterface
    45→
    46→Manages ref-counted middleware subscriptions. A single global `MessageCallback` delivers all incoming messages as `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>` to the `MessageBuffer`. Implementations:
    47→- `Ros2SubscriptionManager`: wraps `GenericSubscriptionManager`. Converts `rclcpp::SerializedMessage` to `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>` via memcpy. Optionally strips large message fields (Image, PointCloud2) via `MessageStripper`.
    48→- `RtiSubscriptionManager`: wraps `DdsSubscriptionManager`. DDS natively produces `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`, no conversion needed.
    49→
    50→### MiddlewareInterface
    51→
    52→Abstract transport layer for client connections. Separates text (request/reply) and binary (per-client push) channels. Single implementation: `WebSocketMiddleware` (IXWebSocket).
    53→
    54→## Core Components
    55→
    56→### BridgeServer
    57→
    58→Main orchestrator. Takes all three interfaces via constructor injection (dependency injection). Does **not** own timers — the entry point drives the event loop by calling three public methods:
    59→- `process_requests()` — polls and handles one JSON API request
    60→- `publish_aggregated_messages()` — drains the message buffer, serializes per subscription group, compresses with ZSTD, sends per-client binary frames
    61→- `check_session_timeouts()` — evicts sessions with expired heartbeats
    62→
    63→Key behaviors:
    64→- **Schema-before-subscribe**: `get_schema()` is called before `subscribe()` to avoid corrupting ref counts if schema extraction fails
    65→- **Additive subscriptions**: `handle_subscribe()` only adds topics, never removes; rate limits can be updated for already-subscribed topics
    66→- **Ref-counted pause/resume**: `handle_pause()` decrements subscription ref counts; `handle_resume()` increments them; `cleanup_session()` skips unsubscribe for paused clients
    67→
    68→### MessageBuffer
    69→
    70→Thread-safe per-topic message buffer. Messages are stored as `BufferedMessage` containing:
    71→- `timestamp_ns` — source-clock timestamp from the middleware
    72→- `received_at_ns` — wall-clock time at insertion (used for TTL cleanup)
    73→- `data` — `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`, CDR-encoded payload
    74→
    75→Stale messages (older than 1 second by `received_at_ns`) are removed on every `add_message()` call. `move_messages()` atomically drains the buffer into the caller's map.
    76→
    77→### SessionManager
    78→
    79→Tracks client sessions by WebSocket connection identity:
    80→- Heartbeat monitoring (default 10-second timeout)
    81→- Per-client subscription tracking with per-topic rate limits
    82→- Pause/resume state
    83→- Thread-safe for concurrent access
    84→
    85→### AggregatedMessageSerializer
    86→
    87→Streaming binary serializer, no middleware dependencies. Per-message wire format:
    88→- Topic name length (uint16_t LE) + topic name (UTF-8)
    89→- Timestamp (uint64_t ns, LE)
    90→- Message data length (uint32_t LE) + message data (CDR)
    91→
    92→`finalize()` produces a 16-byte header (`PJRB` magic, message count, uncompressed size, flags) followed by a ZSTD-compressed payload. See [API docs](API.md#binary-message-format) for full format.
    93→
    94→### WebSocketMiddleware
    95→
    96→IXWebSocket-based implementation of `MiddlewareInterface`:
    97→- Connection-oriented client identity via `connectionState->getId()`
    98→- Incoming text requests queued and dequeued via `receive_request()`
    99→- Per-client binary send for aggregated message frames
   100→- Connect/disconnect callbacks for automatic session lifecycle
   101→
   102→## Event Loop
   103→
   104→BridgeServer exposes methods; the entry point drives timing:
   105→
   106→**ROS2** (`ros2/src/main.cpp`): Three `rclcpp` wall timers:
   107→- 10 ms → `process_requests()`
   108→- 1/publish_rate → `publish_aggregated_messages()`
   109→- 1 s → `check_session_timeouts()`
   110→
   111→Spun via `SingleThreadedExecutor::spin_some(100ms)`.
   112→
   113→**RTI** (`rti/src/main.cpp`): `std::chrono` loop with 1 ms sleep:
   114→- Every iteration → `process_requests()`
   115→- At publish_rate → `publish_aggregated_messages()`
   116→- Every 1 s → `check_session_timeouts()`
   117→- Every 5 s (optional) → stats snapshot
   118→
   119→## ROS2-Specific Components
   120→
   121→- **TopicDiscovery**: Discovers topics via `rclcpp::Node::get_topic_names_and_types()`, filtering system topics
   122→- **SchemaExtractor**: Reads `.msg` files from ROS2 package share directories via `ament_index_cpp`, recursively expanding nested types (matches rosbag2 MCAP format)
   123→- **GenericSubscriptionManager**: Ref-counted `rclcpp::GenericSubscription` per topic
   124→- **MessageStripper**: Strips data fields from large message types (Image, PointCloud2, etc.)
   125→
   126→## RTI-Specific Components
   127→
   128→- **DdsTopicDiscovery**: Discovers topics via DDS participant discovery across configured domain IDs
   129→- **DdsSubscriptionManager**: Manages DDS DataReaders, natively produces `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`
   130→
   131→## Design Decisions
   132→
   133→### Backend-Agnostic Core via Interfaces
   134→`TopicSourceInterface` and `SubscriptionManagerInterface` allow the same `BridgeServer` to work with ROS2 or RTI DDS without compile-time dependencies on either middleware.
   135→
   136→### Message Data as `shared_ptr<vector<byte>>`
   137→Backend-agnostic type eliminates `rclcpp::SerializedMessage` from the core. ROS2 adapter converts via memcpy; RTI adapter already produces this type natively.
   138→
   139→### Externalized Event Loop
   140→BridgeServer has no internal timers. This avoids coupling to any specific executor model (rclcpp timers vs. `std::chrono` loop) and keeps the core library free of middleware dependencies.
   141→
   142→### Shared Subscriptions with Reference Counting
   143→One underlying middleware subscription per topic, shared across all clients. Ref count incremented on subscribe/resume, decremented on unsubscribe/pause/disconnect.
   144→
   145→### TTL Cleanup by Wall-Clock Time
   146→MessageBuffer cleanup uses `received_at_ns` (wall clock), not `timestamp_ns` (source clock). This prevents sim-time offsets from causing premature eviction or unbounded growth.
   147→
   148→### Per-Client Message Filtering
   149→Each client receives only messages for its subscribed topics, with per-topic rate limiting. The server serializes, compresses, and sends individually per client.
   150→
   151→## Thread Safety
   152→
   153→### Lock Ordering (to prevent deadlock)
   154→
   155→```
   156→cleanup_mutex_ > last_sent_mutex_ > stats_mutex_
   157→```
   158→
   159→`SessionManager::mutex_` may be acquired while holding any of these. Never acquire a higher-order lock while holding a lower-order one.
   160→
   161→### Per-Component Guarantees
   162→
   163→- **MessageBuffer**: Internal mutex protects all buffer operations
   164→- **SessionManager**: Internal mutex protects session map
   165→- **GenericSubscriptionManager**: Internal mutex protects subscription map
   166→- **BridgeServer**: `cleanup_mutex_` prevents concurrent cleanup; `last_sent_mutex_` protects rate-limiting state; `stats_mutex_` protects counters. Frames are built under `last_sent_mutex_`, sent outside it (minimizes lock contention)
   167→- **WebSocketMiddleware**: Separate mutexes for state, client map, and message queue
   168→
   169→## Shutdown Sequence
   170→
   171→Both entry points follow the same ordered shutdown to avoid use-after-free:
   172→
   173→1. Cancel timers / exit event loop
   174→2. Clear the subscription manager's message callback (`set_message_callback(nullptr)`)
   175→3. Unsubscribe all middleware subscriptions
   176→4. Shutdown WebSocket server (BridgeServer destructor handles this if not done explicitly)
   177→
   178→## Performance Characteristics
   179→
   180→- **Compression**: ZSTD level 1 (typically 50-70% size reduction)
   181→- **Memory**: Automatic 1-second TTL prevents unbounded growth
   182→- **Concurrent Clients**: 10+ clients supported
   183→

1→# plotjuggler_ros_bridge
     2→
     3→[![ROS2 Humble](https://github.com/PlotJuggler/plotjuggler_ros_bridge/actions/workflows/ros-humble.yaml/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://github.com/PlotJuggler/plotjuggler_ros_bridge/actions/workflows/ros-humble.yaml)
     4→[![ROS2 Jazzy](https://github.com/PlotJuggler/plotjuggler_ros_bridge/actions/workflows/ros-jazzy.yaml/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://github.com/PlotJuggler/plotjuggler_ros_bridge/actions/workflows/ros-jazzy.yaml)
     5→[![ROS2 Rolling](https://github.com/PlotJuggler/plotjuggler_ros_bridge/actions/workflows/ros-rolling.yaml/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://github.com/PlotJuggler/plotjuggler_ros_bridge/actions/workflows/ros-rolling.yaml)
     6→
     7→A high-performance ROS2 bridge server that forwards ROS2 topic content over WebSocket, without requiring DDS on the client side.
     8→
     9→## Overview
    10→
    11→`plotjuggler_ros_bridge` enables clients to subscribe to ROS2 topics and receive aggregated messages at 50 Hz without needing a full ROS2/DDS installation. This is particularly useful for visualization tools like PlotJuggler, remote monitoring applications, and lightweight clients that need access to ROS2 data.
    12→
    13→### Key Features
    14→
    15→- **No DDS Required**: Clients connect via WebSocket (single port) without needing ROS2/DDS installed
    16→- **High Performance**: 50 Hz message aggregation with ZSTD compression
    17→- **Multi-Client Support**: Multiple clients can connect simultaneously with shared subscriptions
    18→- **Session Management**: Automatic cleanup of disconnected clients via heartbeat monitoring and WebSocket close events
    19→- **Runtime Schema Discovery**: Automatic extraction of message schemas from installed ROS2 packages
    20→- **Zero-Copy Design**: Efficient message handling using shared pointers and move semantics
    21→- **Large Message Stripping**: Automatic stripping of large array fields (Image, PointCloud2, LaserScan, OccupancyGrid) to reduce bandwidth while preserving metadata
    22→- **Type-Safe Error Handling**: Comprehensive error reporting using `tl::expected`
    23→
    24→## Architecture
    25→
    26→For detailed architecture documentation, see [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md).
    27→
    28→### Dependencies
    29→
    30→#### ROS2 Packages (Runtime)
    31→- `rclcpp` - ROS2 C++ client library
    32→- `ament_index_cpp` - Locate ROS2 package share directories
    33→
    34→#### System Libraries
    35→- **ZSTD** (libzstd): `sudo apt install libzstd-dev`
    36→
    37→#### Vendored Packages
    38→- **IXWebSocket** (`v11.4.6`): WebSocket server/client
    39→- **nlohmann/json** - JSON library
    40→- **tl/expected** - Type-safe error handling
    41→
    42→## Installation
    43→
    44→### Building from Source
    45→
    46→```bash
    47→# 1. Create workspace (if needed)
    48→mkdir -p ~/ws_plotjuggler/src
    49→cd ~/ws_plotjuggler/src
    50→
    51→# 2. Clone the repository
    52→git clone <repository_url> plotjuggler_ros_bridge
    53→
    54→# 3. Install system dependencies
    55→sudo apt install libzstd-dev
    56→
    57→# 4. Source ROS2
    58→source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
    59→
    60→# 5. Build the package
    61→cd ~/ws_plotjuggler
    62→colcon build --packages-select plotjuggler_ros_bridge --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
    63→
    64→# 6. Source the workspace
    65→source install/setup.bash
    66→```
    67→
    68→### Running Tests
    69→
    70→```bash
    71→# Run all unit tests (150 tests)
    72→colcon test --packages-select plotjuggler_ros_bridge
    73→
    74→# View test results
    75→colcon test-result --verbose
    76→```
    77→
    78→## Usage
    79→
    80→### Starting the Server
    81→
    82→#### Basic Usage (Default Configuration)
    83→
    84→```bash
    85→source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
    86→source ~/ws_plotjuggler/install/setup.bash
    87→ros2 run plotjuggler_ros_bridge plotjuggler_ros_bridge_node
    88→```
    89→
    90→Default configuration:
    91→- WebSocket port: 8080
    92→- Publish rate: 50 Hz
    93→- Session timeout: 10 seconds
    94→
    95→#### Custom Configuration
    96→
    97→```bash
    98→ros2 run plotjuggler_ros_bridge plotjuggler_ros_bridge_node --ros-args \
    99→  -p port:=9090 \
   100→  -p publish_rate:=30.0 \
   101→  -p session_timeout:=15.0
   102→```
   103→
   104→### Configuration Parameters
   105→
   106→| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
   107→|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
   108→| `port` | int | 8080 | WebSocket server port |
   109→| `publish_rate` | double | 50.0 | Aggregation publish rate in Hz |
   110→| `session_timeout` | double | 10.0 | Client timeout duration in seconds |
   111→| `strip_large_messages` | bool | true | Strip large arrays from Image, PointCloud2, LaserScan, OccupancyGrid messages |
   112→
   113→### Testing with Sample Data
   114→
   115→```bash
   116→# Terminal 1: Play rosbag
   117→source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
   118→ros2 bag play /path/to/sample.mcap --loop
   119→
   120→# Terminal 2: Run server
   121→source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
   122→source ~/ws_plotjuggler/install/setup.bash
   123→ros2 run plotjuggler_ros_bridge plotjuggler_ros_bridge_node
   124→
   125→# Terminal 3: Run Python test client
   126→cd ~/ws_plotjuggler/src/plotjuggler_ros_bridge
   127→python3 tests/integration/test_client.py --subscribe /topic1 /topic2
   128→```
   129→
   130→## API Protocol
   131→
   132→For the full API protocol documentation (commands, responses, binary wire format), see [docs/API.md](docs/API.md).
   133→
   134→## Troubleshooting
   135→
   136→### Server fails to start with "Failed to listen on port"
   137→
   138→Another process is using the port. Either kill the conflicting process or use a custom port:
   139→
   140→```bash
   141→ros2 run plotjuggler_ros_bridge plotjuggler_ros_bridge_node --ros-args -p port:=9090
   142→```
   143→
   144→### Client receives no data
   145→
   146→1. Verify server is running: `ps aux | grep plotjuggler_ros_bridge`
   147→2. Check topics are being published: `ros2 topic list`
   148→3. Verify heartbeat is being sent (required every 1 second)
   149→4. Check server logs: `ros2 run plotjuggler_ros_bridge plotjuggler_ros_bridge_node --ros-args --log-level debug`
   150→
   151→### "Failed to get schema for topic" error
   152→
   153→The message type's .msg file was not found. Ensure the ROS2 package containing the message type is installed and sourced:
   154→
   155→```bash
   156→ros2 interface show <package_name>/msg/<MessageType>
   157→```
   158→
   159→### Session timeout / Automatic unsubscription
   160→
   161→The client stopped sending heartbeats. Ensure the client sends a heartbeat every 1 second. The default timeout is 10 seconds. Increase if needed:
   162→
   163→```bash
   164→ros2 run plotjuggler_ros_bridge plotjuggler_ros_bridge_node --ros-args -p session_timeout:=20.0
   165→```
   166→
